generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OperationalLogCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects operational logs and contextual data traces from the physical engineered system for digital shadow aggregation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalLogDataTransmitted
]

instance SensorDataCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects sensor readings and environmental parameters from physical machinery and equipment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SensorDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OperationalLogDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalLogCollector
    base:desc "Transmits aggregated operational logs and contextual data traces from physical system to digital shadow layer"
]

instance SensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SensorDataCollector
    base:desc "Transmits raw sensor readings and environmental parameters from physical system components"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DigitalShadowDataAggregationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "UML-class-based framework implementing the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Fig. 1) for data aggregation and abstraction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowDataAggregatorEnabler
]

instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model capturing system functions across abstraction layers (requirements, functional, logical)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelProcessorEnabler
]

instance SysMLLogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based logical architecture model defining component interfaces and interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelProcessorEnabler
]

instance MontiArcComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture model defining components, connectors, and composition rules for system decomposition"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcModelProcessorEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model capturing dynamic system behavior and physics-based simulations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkIntegrationEnabler
]

instance StateMachineBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML state machine model (v1.7+) capturing discrete behavioral logic and transitions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelProcessorEnabler
]

instance CppControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "C++-based control logic model implementing custom algorithms and optimization routines"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CppIntegrationEnabler
]

instance JavaAnalysisLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Java-based analytical logic model for data processing and predictive analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo JavaIntegrationEnabler
]

instance AggregatedOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured operational data aggregated from raw logs through digital shadow abstraction process"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalLogDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowDataAggregatorEnabler, DataInterpretationEnabler
]

instance ProcessedSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized and filtered sensor data prepared for behavioral modeling and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataInterpretationEnabler, SimulationInputPreprocessorEnabler
]

instance ModelIntegrationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unified data representation combining heterogeneous model outputs (SysML, ADLs, simulation results)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelIntegrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DigitalShadowDataAggregatorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component implementing digital shadow data aggregation and abstraction logic according to Digital Shadow Reference Model"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowDataAggregationService
]

instance SysMLModelProcessorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Toolchain integration component processing SysML models (functional/logical architectures, state machines) for system analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemArchitectureAnalysisService, BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance MontiArcModelProcessorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc-specific processor handling component-connector models and automaton-based behavioral descriptions"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentInteractionAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bridge component enabling Simulink model integration with other DT components for hybrid simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsBasedSimulationService
]

instance CppIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration layer for C++-based control logic models enabling real-time execution and parameter adjustment"
    DTDFVocab:enables ClosedLoopControlService
]

instance JavaIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Java runtime environment for executing analytical logic models and data processing routines"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalysisService
]

instance DataInterpretationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Active processing component interpreting digital shadow data to derive actionable insights"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataInterpretationService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance ModelIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous model integration framework combining outputs from SysML, ADLs, and simulation tools"
    DTDFVocab:enables UnifiedVisualizationService
]

instance SimulationInputPreprocessorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Preprocessing component preparing sensor data for simulation inputs with normalization and filtering"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsBasedSimulationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DigitalShadowDataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive service collecting and abstracting raw operational data from physical system into structured digital shadow format"
    DTDFVocab:provides DigitalShadowDataInsight
]

instance SystemArchitectureAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing system architecture through SysML functional and logical models to identify structural patterns"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemArchitectureInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing behavioral simulations using SysML state machines and MontiArc automata"
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralSimulationInsight
]

instance ComponentInteractionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing component interactions and interface definitions using MontiArc component-connector models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentInteractionInsight
]

instance PhysicsBasedSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing physics-based simulations using MathWorks Simulink models integrated with sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides PhysicsSimulationInsight
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling bidirectional control between digital twin and physical system via C++-based logic"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing predictive analysis using Java-based analytical models on processed data"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveInsight
]

instance DataInterpretationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service actively interpreting digital shadow data to derive operational insights and recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataInterpretationInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying anomalies in system operation through pattern recognition on processed data"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance UnifiedVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating heterogeneous model outputs into unified views for multi-stakeholder analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides UnifiedVisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DigitalShadowDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured operational data representation enabling passive monitoring of physical system state"
]

instance SystemArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified structural patterns and architectural relationships across system abstraction layers"
]

instance BehavioralSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results showing system behavior under various operational conditions"
]

instance ComponentInteractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of component interactions and interface definitions with potential bottlenecks identified"
]

instance PhysicsSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation results predicting system performance under different scenarios"
]

instance DataInterpretationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

instance PredictiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

instance UnifiedVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

instance ControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control actions executed on physical system based on digital twin recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ClosedLoopController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Component implementing closed-loop control logic between digital twin and physical system"
]

// C1: Environment
instance EngineeredSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Complex engineered system environment (e.g., manufacturing/industrial system) requiring multi-abstraction modeling"
    base:contains OperationalLogCollector, SensorDataCollector,
                 DigitalShadowDataAggregatorEnabler, SysMLModelProcessorEnabler,
                 MontiArcModelProcessorEnabler, SimulinkIntegrationEnabler,
                 CppIntegrationEnabler, JavaIntegrationEnabler,
                 DataInterpretationEnabler, ModelIntegrationEnabler,
                 SimulationInputPreprocessorEnabler,
                 ClosedLoopController
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration and coordination are implicitly tied to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks, which integrate heterogeneous modeling notations (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink/MathWorks for behavior, or general-purpose languages like C++/Java for implementation). The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) emphasizes **one-way data flow** for digital shadows, contrasting with Digital Twins, which require **active components** for data interpretation and processing. Orchestration may leverage **ADLs (e.g., MontiArc Automaton, SysML state machines)** for behavioral coordination, while **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) or **code-based implementations** handle dynamic system interactions. Resource management and system-wide coordination are not explicitly detailed, but the integration of **multiple tools and stakeholders** suggests a **hybrid architecture** combining model-driven and code-driven approaches."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **fidelity and validity** are governed by:
  - **Model fidelity requirements**: Ensured through **multi-abstraction layer modeling** (e.g., SysML’s logical/physical layers) and **validation against physical system behavior** via simulation tools (e.g., Simulink). Fidelity is implicitly tied to the **granularity of ADLs** (e.g., MontiArc Automaton) and **code-level implementations** (C++/Java), which must align with real-world dynamics.
  - **Validation and verification (V&V) methods**: Leveraging **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., OOSEM, SPES) to cross-validate models across abstraction layers. Behavioral models (e.g., state machines in SysML) are verified via **simulation-driven testing** ([29], [30]) and **code integration** (e.g., C++/Java implementations).
  - **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Stem from **data abstraction** in digital shadows (e.g., aggregated traces may lose granularity) and **model-simulation discrepancies** (e.g., Simulink vs. real-world physics). Quantification relies on **statistical analysis** of data traces and **comparative studies** between digital and physical states.
  - **Error detection and correction**: Implemented via **active components** in the DT (e.g., [35]–[37]) that process incoming data from digital shadows and **adapt models dynamically**. Correction mechanisms may include **recalibration of simulation parameters** or **model refinement** using tools like Simulink.
  - **Quality assurance processes**: Enforced through **stakeholder integration** (heterogeneous perspectives in MBSE) and **methodological rigor** (e.g., RFLP’s Physics layer validation). The DT’s **active processing** ensures continuous alignment with physical system states, mitigating drift in fidelity over time."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** [32] implies that the infrastructure must support **one-way data flows** from physical to digital systems, which may require **reliable communication protocols** (e.g., industrial protocols like OPC UA, MQTT, or HTTP/REST for IoT devices). While **network topology** and **architecture** (e.g., star, mesh, or hierarchical) are unspecified, the integration of **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, or C++/Java-based systems) suggests a **hybrid infrastructure** capable of bridging disparate systems. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, but the need for **real-time or near-real-time data aggregation** (as implied by the Digital Shadow’s passive collection) may demand low-latency connections. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not described, though the passive nature of the Digital Shadow implies fault tolerance may be handled externally. **Security protocols** (e.g., TLS, VPNs, or industrial cybersecurity frameworks) are not mentioned, but the integration of **active DT components** (as opposed to passive shadows) in [35]–[37] suggests a need for secure authentication and validation."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the integration of **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, or general-purpose languages like C++/Java) implies support for **multi-platform deployment**, potentially including **cloud, edge, or on-premise hosting**. **Computational and storage requirements** are undefined, though the use of **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) and **modeling languages** (e.g., SysML) suggests demand for **high-performance computing** for complex behavioral models. **Scalability mechanisms** are not specified, but the mention of **heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives** [31] implies a need for modular or microservice-based architectures. **Deployment models** (e.g., containerized, virtualized, or hybrid) are not described, nor are **availability/reliability assurances** (e.g., SLAs, redundancy, or failover strategies). The document does not provide details on **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes) or **infrastructure-as-code** tools."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the Digital Twin system is implied through the use of **interoperable modeling languages and tools** within MBSE. The document highlights the need to integrate **heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives**, requiring **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** across tools like SysML, Simulink, and general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java). While no specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, MES) are mentioned, the reliance on **ADLs (e.g., AADL, MontiArc)** for component interfaces and connectors suggests **standardized integration patterns** (e.g., component-based or service-oriented architectures). Dependencies on external systems are inferred from the **passive-to-active data flow** distinction in Digital Shadows vs. Twins, but concrete integration mechanisms remain unspecified."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) standards**, including **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** for system architecture and behavioral modeling. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** provide standardized component-connector-based system descriptions. **Interoperability** is addressed through integration with **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) and **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., C++, Java), though no specific interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE, OMG DDS) are mentioned. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) implies compliance with **manufacturing-specific data aggregation and abstraction standards**, but no explicit certification frameworks or industry-specific standards (e.g., ISO 23247 for Digital Twins, IEC 62424 for industrial systems) are referenced. The document does not detail **compliance frameworks** or **certification requirements** for the Digital Twin system."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the integration of a **safety cell** that restricts the operational range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]** to prevent collisions or unsafe movements. The system enforces **fail-safe mechanisms** by limiting the maximum achievable speed to **|q̇_max| = 75 [°/s]**, ensuring controlled motion dynamics. **Cybersecurity protocols** are implicitly implied via the use of the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, which is designed for industrial automation and adheres to **IEC 61131-3** and **IEC 62061** safety standards for machine control systems. **Access control mechanisms** are likely enforced at the controller level, where only authorized personnel can modify operational parameters (e.g., position, speed, or acceleration inputs). **Threat mitigation strategies** are partially addressed through the **dual-encoder sensor arrangement** (motor encoder + secondary encoder behind the gearbox), which reduces **backlash and positional uncertainty** by cross-referencing encoder data to infer drive train elasticity and friction. This redundancy enhances **fault tolerance** and **data integrity** for critical motion control signals. No explicit mention of **authentication systems**, **consent management**, or **data anonymization** is provided in the document."
]
